entries.english: That Blessed One is an arahant (a fully awakened one), perfectly awakened.
The Dhamma (the Buddha's teaching) is well expounded by that Blessed One.
The Sangha (the community of the Blessed One's disciples) has practiced well.
To that Blessed One, to the true Dhamma, and to the Sangha
we offer these offerings, rightly arranged, and pay deep homage.
It is well, Venerable Sir, that the Blessed One, though long since attained to final Nibbāna
out of compassion for later generations,
please accept these humble, trifling offerings,
for our long-lasting welfare and happiness.
The Blessed One is an arahant, perfectly awakened;
To the Buddha, the Blessed One, I pay homage.

entries.pali: Svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo dhammaṃ namassāmi --- bow ---
entries.english: The Dhamma is well expounded by the Blessed One; to the Dhamma I pay homage.
The Sangha of the Blessed One’s disciples has practiced well; to the Sangha I pay homage.

entries.pali: Handa mayaṃ buddhassa bhagavato pubbabhāganamakāraṃ karoma se
entries.english: Now let us pay preliminary homage to the Buddha, the Blessed One. Homage to that Blessed One, an arahant, perfectly awakened.
Now let us recite in praise of the Buddha.
He is the Tathāgata (the Thus-Gone One), an arahant, perfectly awakened,
endowed with true knowledge and conduct, the Well-gone One, knower of the worlds,
unsurpassed trainer of persons to be tamed,
teacher of gods and humans; the awakened, Blessed One.

entries.pali: Yo imaṃ lokaṃ sadevakaṃ samārakaṃ sabrahmakaṃ
entries.english: He has realized this world with its gods, Māras, and Brahmās,
and this generation with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans,
and teaches it after having realized it by his own direct knowledge.
He teaches the Dhamma that is admirable in the beginning,
admirable in the middle, and admirable in the end,
with the meaning and wording complete, perfect in every respect, wholly pure.
He has made known the perfectly complete holy life.
To that Blessed One I pay deep homage.
To that Blessed One I bow my head.
Now let us recite in praise of the Dhamma.
The Dhamma is well expounded by the Blessed One,
visible here and now, timeless, inviting one to come and see,
leading inwards, to be realized individually by the wise.
To this Dhamma I pay deep homage.
To this Dhamma I bow my head.
Now let us recite in praise of the Sangha.
The Sangha of the Blessed One’s disciples has practiced well,
has practiced uprightly,
has practiced in the right way,
has practiced properly,
namely, the four pairs, the eight kinds of noble individuals.
This is the Sangha of the Blessed One’s disciples:
worthy of gifts, worthy of hospitality, worthy of offerings, worthy of reverential salutation,
the unsurpassed field of merit for the world.
To this Sangha I pay deep homage.
To this Sangha I bow my head.

chapters.title: Ratanattayappaṇāmagāthā
chapters.titleEnglish: Verses of Reverence to the Triple Gem

entries.pali: Handa mayaṃ ratanattayappaṇāmagāthāyo ceva saṃvegaparikittanapāṭhañca bhaṇāma se
entries.english: Now let us recite our verses of salutation to the Triple Gem and a passage to stir urgency.
The Buddha, utterly pure, with compassion deep as the ocean,
possessing the eye of excellent, stainless wisdom,
the destroyer of the world’s evil and defilements:
that Buddha I revere with a devoted heart.
The Dhamma of that Teacher, like a lamp,
distinguished as the Path, Fruition, the Deathless and its divisions,
supramundane, illuminating its own objective,
that Dhamma I revere with a devoted heart.
The Sangha, known as a fertile field and a field of fearlessness,
the seers who realize, awakened in the footsteps of the Well-Gone One,
free from fickleness, noble and of excellent wisdom:
that Sangha I revere with a devoted heart.
Thus this threefold basis, supremely worthy of veneration,
when honored and attended to in this way,
may the merit I have made cause all my misfortunes
not to come to be, through the fulfillment of its power.

entries.pali: Idha tathāgato loke uppanno arahaṃ sammāsambuddho,
entries.english: Here in this world the Tathāgata, an arahant, perfectly awakened, has arisen.

entries.pali: Dhammo ca desito niyyāniko upasamiko parinibbāniko sambodhagāmī sugatappavedito.
entries.english: And the Dhamma has been taught that leads out, brings peace, leads to final Nibbāna, leads to awakening, proclaimed by the Well-gone One.
Having heard that Dhamma, we understand thus:

entries.pali: Jātipi dukkhā, jarāpi dukkhā, maraṇampi dukkhaṃ,
entries.english: birth is suffering, aging is suffering, death is suffering;
sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief and despair are suffering;

entries.pali: Appiyehi sampayogo dukkho, piyehi vippayogo dukkho,
entries.english: association with the disliked is suffering, separation from the loved is suffering;
not getting what one wants is also suffering.
In brief, the five aggregates subject to clinging are suffering.

entries.pali: Seyyathīdaṃ, rūpūpādānakkhandho vedanūpādānakkhandho saññūpādānakkhandho saṅkhārūpādānakkhandho viññāṇūpādānakkhandho,
entries.english: That is to say: the aggregate of clinging to form, to feeling, to perception, to formations, and to consciousness.

entries.pali: yesaṃ pariññāya dharamāno so bhagavā evaṃ bahulaṃ sāvake vineti.
entries.english: For the full understanding of these, while still alive, the Blessed One often trains his disciples in this way.

entries.pali: Evaṃbhāgā ca panassa bhagavato sāvakesu anusāsanī bahulā pavattati.
entries.english: And in this way his instruction among the disciples frequently proceeds.

entries.pali: Rūpaṃ aniccaṃ, vedanā aniccā, saññā aniccā, saṅkhārā aniccā, viññāṇaṃ aniccaṃ,
entries.english: Form is impermanent, feeling is impermanent, perception is impermanent, formations are impermanent, consciousness is impermanent;

entries.pali: rūpaṃ anattā, vedanā anattā, saññā anattā, saṅkhārā anattā, viññāṇaṃ anattā,
entries.english: form is not-self, feeling is not-self, perception is not-self, formations are not-self, consciousness is not-self;

entries.pali: sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā, sabbe dhammā anattāti.
entries.english: all conditioned things are impermanent, all dhammas are not-self.

entries.pali: Te to Tā. mayaṃ, otiṇṇāmaha jātiyā jarāmaraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
entries.english: We, who are sunk in birth, aging and death, in sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief and despair,

entries.pali: Dukkhotiṇṇā dukkhaparetā, appevanāmimassa kevalassa dukkhakkhandhassa antakiriyā paññāyethāti.
entries.english: overwhelmed by suffering and oppressed by suffering, may the ending of this entire mass of suffering become apparent to us.

entries.pali: Ciraparinibbutampi taṃ bhagavantaṃ saraṇaṃ gatā, dhammañca saṅghañca,
entries.english: Though that Blessed One has long attained final Nibbāna, we have gone for refuge to that Blessed One, to the Dhamma and to the Saṅgha,

entries.pali: tassa bhagavato sāsanaṃ yathāsati yathābalaṃ manasikaroma anupaṭipajjāma.
entries.english: and we attend to that Blessed One’s teaching and practise it according to our mindfulness and strength.

entries.pali: Sā sā no paṭipatti imassa kevalassa dukkhakkhandassa antakiriyāya saṃvattatu.
entries.english: May this practice of ours lead to the ending of this entire mass of suffering.

chapters.title: Taṅkhaṇika-paccavekkhaṇa-pāṭho
chapters.titleEnglish: Reflection at the Time of Use

entries.english: Now let us recite the reflection at the time of using the requisites.
Wisely reflecting, I use the robe,
only for warding off cold,
for warding off heat,
for warding off the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and creeping things,
only for the purpose of covering the body.
Wisely reflecting, I use almsfood:
not for fun,
not for intoxication,
not for fattening,
not for beautification,
but only for the maintenance and nourishment of this body,
for keeping it unharmed and for supporting the holy life, thinking:
'thus I shall allay old feeling,
and not arouse new feeling;
there will be for me support for the journey of life,
and blamelessness and comfortable abiding.
Wisely reflecting, I use a lodging,
only for warding off cold,
for warding off heat,
for warding off the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and creeping things,
only for dispelling the hardships of climate and for the enjoyment of seclusion.
Wisely reflecting, I use medicinal requisites for the sick,
only for warding off painful feelings that have arisen,
and for the attainment of freedom from affliction.

chapters.title: Dhātupaṭikūla-paccavekkhaṇa-pāṭho
chapters.titleEnglish: Reflection on the Repulsiveness of the Elements

entries.english: Now let us recite the contemplation on the repulsiveness of the elements.
Arising according to conditions, this is merely a mass of elements,
namely this robe,

entries.pali: tadupabhuñjako ca puggalo dhātumattako nissatto nijjīvo suñño,
entries.english: and the person who makes use of it is also just elements, devoid of self, lifeless, empty,
yet all these robes themselves are not disgusting

entries.pali: imaṃ pūtikāyaṃ patvā ativiya jigucchaniyāni jāyanti.
entries.english: but, when applied to this putrid body, they become exceedingly disgusting.
Arising according to conditions, this is merely a mass of elements,
namely this almsfood,

entries.pali: tadupabhuñjako ca puggalo dhātumattako nissatto nijjīvo suñño,
entries.english: and the person who eats it is also just elements, devoid of self, lifeless, empty,
yet this whole almsfood itself is not disgusting

entries.pali: imaṃ pūtikāyaṃ patvā ativiya jigucchaniyo jāyati.
entries.english: but, on reaching this putrid body, it becomes exceedingly disgusting.
Arising according to conditions, this is merely a mass of elements,
namely this lodging,

entries.pali: tadupabhuñjako ca puggalo dhātumattako nissatto nijjīvo suñño,
entries.english: and the person who uses it is also just elements, devoid of self, lifeless, empty,
yet all these lodgings themselves are not disgusting

entries.pali: imaṃ pūtikāyaṃ patvā ativiya jigucchaniyāni jāyanti.
entries.english: but, in connection with this putrid body, they become exceedingly disgusting.
Arising according to conditions, this is merely a mass of elements,
namely medicines and requisites for the sick,

entries.pali: tadupabhuñjako ca puggalo dhātumattako nissatto nijjīvo suñño,
entries.english: and the person who uses them is also just elements, devoid of self, lifeless, empty,
yet this whole support of medicines for the sick is itself not disgusting

entries.pali: imaṃ pūtikāyaṃ patvā ativiya jigucchaniyo jāyati.
entries.english: but, when it comes to this putrid body, it becomes exceedingly disgusting.

entries.pali: Handa mayaṃ pattidāna gāthāyo bhaṇāma se
entries.english: Now let us recite the verses for sharing merit.
May the devas who dwell in this monastery,
in the stupa, the buildings, and the Bodhi-tree enclosure, here and there,
be honoured through this gift of Dhamma,
and bring safety and well-being to this monastery compound.

entries.pali: therā ca majjhā navakā ca bhikkhavo,
entries.english: May elder, intermediate, and newly ordained monks,
temple attendants, donors, and lay followers,

entries.pali: gāmā ca desā nigamā ca issarā,
entries.english: villages, districts, towns, and their rulers,
and all beings endowed with life, be happy.
Those born from the womb and those who come from an egg,
those born from moisture, or who arise spontaneously,
having relied on the excellent Dhamma that leads out of suffering,
may they all bring about the ending of suffering.

entries.pali: thātu ciraṃ sataṃ dhammo, dhammaddharā ca puggalā,
entries.english: May the Dhamma of the good endure for a long time, together with the individuals who uphold the Dhamma.

entries.pali: saṅgho hotu samaggo va, atthāya ca hitāya ca,
entries.english: May the Sangha remain harmonious, for welfare and for benefit.
May the true Dhamma protect us and all who practise in accordance with the Dhamma.
May we attain growth and fulfilment in the Dhamma taught and proclaimed by the Noble Ones.
Now let us recite the Buddha's words of advice and instruction.
Patience is the supreme purifying practice,
Nibbāna is supreme, say the Buddhas.

entries.pali: Na hi pabbajito parūpaghātī, samaṇo hoti paraṃ viheṭhayanto.
entries.english: A mendicant does not harm others, a recluse oppresses no one.
This is the teaching of all Buddhas.
Abstaining from all evil,
cultivating the wholesome,
cleansing of one's own mind,
This is the teaching of all Buddhas.
not insulting,
not harming,
carefulness in the Fundamental Precepts,
moderation in food,
dwelling in solitude,
engaging in higher mental development,
This is the teaching of all Buddhas.
All beings: All beings are our companions, born, growing old, falling ill, and dying together.
free from enmity: May we truly be happy and never use violence against one another.
free from ill will: May we truly be happy and never injure or oppress each other.
free from affliction: May we truly be happy and have no suffering of body or mind at all.
May they dwell in happiness: May you have happiness of body and mind, protecting yourselves from all suffering; may those who suffer find happiness, and may those who are happy grow even happier.
all beings: All beings who are egg-born, womb-born, moisture-born, or spontaneously born: may they receive this wholesome merit and blessing, each and every one.

chapters.title: Ratanattayanāmakārapātha
footerNotes: This saying shows respect for the Triple Gem, firms faith in it, and keeps a close relationship with it in mind. It serves as a preliminary step that will make the attainment of Dhammakaya (the Body of Truth) more accessible.

entries.pali: Arahaṃ sammāsambuddho bhagavā, buddhaṃ bhagavantaṃ abhivādemi.

entries.english: The Buddha is my refuge.”
The Dhamma is my refuge.”
The Saṅgha is my refuge.”
For translation, see page sec:m01 (Morning Chanting)
For translation, see page sec:m02 (Morning Chanting)

entries.pali: Handadāni mayaṃ taṃ bhagavantaṃ vācāya abhigāyituṃ pubbabhāganamakārañceva buddhānussatinayañca karomase
entries.english: Now, in order to praise the Blessed One with speech, we perform the preliminary homage and the method of recollecting the Buddha.
Homage to the Blessed One, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One.

entries.pali: Taṃ kho pana bhagavantaṃ evaṃ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:
entries.english: Now, about that Blessed One, this fine report has spread abroad:

entries.pali: Iti pi so bhagavā arahaṃ sammāsambuddho
entries.english: “Such indeed is that Blessed One: an arahant, perfectly awakened;
accomplished in knowledge and conduct, well-gone, knower of the worlds, unsurpassed;
trainer of persons to be tamed, teacher of devas and humans, the Buddha, the Blessed One.”

chapters.title: Buddhābhigīti
chapters.titleEnglish: Chant in Praise of the Buddha

entries.english: Now let us chant in praise of the Buddha.
Endowed with the noble qualities of the Buddha, the Arahant, and the Excellent Ones,
his being complete with pure insight and compassion,

entries.pali: Bodhesi yo sujanataṃ kamalaṃ va sūro
entries.english: he awakened the good people, like the sun [awakens] the lotus,
I bow with my head to that peaceful one, the Lord of Conquerors.
The Buddha is the secure, supreme refuge for all living beings.
As the first object of recollection, I bow to him with my head.

entries.pali: Buddhassāhaṃ dāso va Buddho me sāmikissaro
entries.english: I am the Buddha's servant; the Buddha is my lord and master.

entries.pali: Buddho dukkhassa ghātā ca Vidhātā ca hitassa me
entries.english: The Buddha destroys suffering and brings about my welfare.
To the Buddha I dedicate this body and this very life.
Paying homage, I will live (practice) in accordance with the Buddha’s clearly realized Dhamma.

entries.pali: Natthi me saraṇaṃ aññaṃ Buddho me saraṇaṃ varaṃ
entries.english: I have no other refuge; the Buddha is my supreme refuge.
By this truthful utterance, may I grow in the Teacher’s dispensation.

entries.pali: Buddhaṃ me vandamānena Yaṃ puññaṃ pasutaṃ idha
entries.english: By my paying homage to the Buddha, whatever merit is produced here,

entries.pali: Sabbepi antarāyā me Māhesuṃ tassa tejasā
entries.english: may all obstacles not afflict me through the power of his radiance.
By body, speech, or mind,
whatever wrong I have done toward the Buddha,
may the Buddha accept my confession,
so that in the future I may restrain myself with regard to the Buddha.
Now let us chant in recollection of the Dhamma.
The Dhamma is well-expounded by the Blessed One,
visible here and now, timeless, inviting one to come and see,
leading inward, to be personally known by the wise.

chapters.title: Dhammābhigīti
chapters.titleEnglish: Chant in Praise of the Dhamma

entries.english: Now let us recite a chant in praise of the Dhamma.
The Dhamma is supreme, endowed with such qualities as being well expounded,
distinguished as Path, Fruit, Study, and Liberation.
The Dhamma prevents the fall into the bad world and supports those who uphold it.
I revere that excellent Dhamma which dispels darkness.
The Dhamma is for all beings the supreme, secure refuge.
It is the second foundation of recollection; to it I pay homage with my head.

entries.pali: Dhammassāhasmi dāso va Dhammo me sāmikissaro
entries.english: I am a servant of the Dhamma; the Dhamma is my lord and master.

entries.pali: Dhammo dukkhassa ghātā ca Vidhātā ca hitassa me
entries.english: The Dhamma is the destroyer of suffering and the establisher of my welfare.
To the Dhamma I entrust this body and this life.
Paying homage, I will live devoted only to the true nature of the Dhamma.

entries.pali: Natthi me saraṇaṃ aññaṃ Dhammo me saraṇaṃ varaṃ
entries.english: I have no other refuge; the Dhamma is my excellent refuge.
By this truthful utterance, may I grow in the Teacher's dispensation.

entries.pali: Dhammaṃ me vandamānena Yaṃ puññaṃ pasutaṃ idha
entries.english: Whatever merit I have gathered here by revering the Dhamma,

entries.pali: Sabbepi antarāyā me Māhesuṃ tassa tejasā
entries.english: may all my obstacles be dispelled through its power.
Now let us chant in recollection of the Saṅgha.
The Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples has practiced well.
The Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples has practiced uprightly.
The Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples has practiced rightly.
The Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples has practiced properly.
That is to say, the four pairs of persons, the eight kinds of individuals.
This is the Saṅgha of the Blessed One’s disciples.
Worthy of gifts, worthy of hospitality, worthy of offerings, worthy of reverential salutation.
The unsurpassed field of merit for the world.

chapters.title: Saṅghābhigīti
chapters.titleEnglish: Chant in Praise of the Sangha

entries.english: Now let us recite a chant in praise of the Saṅgha.
Born of the True Dhamma, endowed with virtues such as good practice,
the noble Saṅgha, supreme among the eight kinds of noble persons,
with body and mind established in the excellent abode of virtue and other qualities,
I revere that pure and noble assembly.
The Saṅgha is for all beings the supreme, secure refuge.
As the third foundation of recollection, I bow to it with my head.

entries.pali: Saṅghassāhasmi dāso va Saṅgho me sāmikissaro
entries.english: I am a servant of the Saṅgha; the Saṅgha is my lord and master.

entries.pali: Saṅgho dukkhassa ghātā ca Vidhātā ca hitassa me
entries.english: The Saṅgha destroys suffering and brings about my welfare.
To the Saṅgha I dedicate this body and this very life.
Paying homage, I will live in accordance with the Saṅgha’s good practice.

entries.pali: Natthi me saraṇaṃ aññaṃ Saṅgho me saraṇaṃ varaṃ
entries.english: I have no other refuge; the Saṅgha is my excellent refuge.
By this truthful utterance, may I grow in the Teacher’s dispensation.

entries.pali: Saṅghaṃ me vandamānena Yaṃ puññaṃ pasutaṃ idha
entries.english: By my paying homage to the Saṅgha, whatever merit is produced here,

entries.pali: Sabbepi antarāyā me Māhesuṃ tassa tejasā
entries.english: may all my obstacles be dispelled through its power.
By body, speech, or mind,
whatever wrong I have done toward the Saṅgha,
may the Saṅgha accept my confession,
so that in the future I may restrain myself with regard to the Saṅgha.
Now let us recite the reflection on past use.

entries.pali: Ajja mayā apaccavekkhitvā yaṃ cīvaraṃ paribhuttaṃ,
entries.english: Today, without proper reflection, I used a robe;
only to ward off cold,
to ward off heat,
to ward off contact with flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and creeping things,
and only for the purpose of covering the private parts.

entries.pali: Ajja mayā apaccavekkhitvā yo piṇḍapāto paribhutto,
entries.english: Today, without proper reflection, I consumed almsfood;

entries.pali: so neva davāya na madāya na maṇḍanāya na vibhūsanāya,
entries.english: not for amusement, not for intoxication, not for adornment, not for beautification,

entries.pali: yāvadeva imassa kāyassa ṭhitiyā yāpanāya vihiṃsuparatiyā brahmacariyānuggahāya,
entries.english: but only for the maintenance and nourishment of this body, for ending harm, and for supporting the holy life;

entries.pali: iti purāṇañca vedanaṃ paṭihaṅkhāmi navañca vedanaṃ na uppādessāmi,
entries.english: thus I shall allay old feelings and not arouse new feelings,

entries.pali: yātrā ca me bhavissati anavajjatā ca phāsuvihāro cāti.
entries.english: and I shall live blamelessly and at ease.

entries.pali: Ajja mayā apaccavekkhitvā yaṃ senāsanaṃ paribhuttaṃ,
entries.english: Today, without proper reflection, I used lodging;
only to ward off cold,
to ward off heat,
to ward off contact with flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and creeping things,
only for the removal of discomforts due to the seasons and for the delight of seclusion.

entries.pali: Ajja mayā apaccavekkhitvā yo gilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhāro paribhutto,
entries.english: Today, without proper reflection, I used medicines and requisites for the sick;

entries.pali: so yāvadeva uppannānaṃ veyyābādhikānaṃ vedanānaṃ paṭighātāya,
entries.english: only to ward off arisen painful feelings,
and for the utmost freedom from affliction.
Now let us recite the verses of dedication.
By this meritorious deed, may my preceptors of superior virtue [benefit].

entries.pali: Ācariyūpakārā ca Mātā pitā ca ñātakā piyā mamaṃ
entries.english: May my teachers and benefactors, my mother and father, and my dear relatives [benefit].

entries.pali: Suriyo candimā rājā Guṇavantā narāpi ca
entries.english: The sun, the moon, kings, and virtuous people as well,

entries.pali: Brahmamārā ca indā ca Lokapālā ca devatā
entries.english: the Brahmās, Māras, Indas, guardian deities, and gods,

entries.pali: Yamo mittā manussā ca Majjhattā verikāpi ca
entries.english: Yama, friends, human beings, the impartial, and even enemies,

entries.pali: Sabbe sattā sukhī hontu Puññāni pakatāni me
entries.english: may all beings be happy through the merits I have made.

entries.pali: Sukhaṃ ca tividhaṃ dentu Khippaṃ pāpetha vomataṃ
entries.english: May they bestow the threefold happiness and quickly reach Nibbāna.
By this meritorious deed and by this dedication,
may I swiftly and easily attain the cutting off of craving and clinging.

entries.pali: Ye santāne hinā dhammā Yāva nibbānato mamaṃ
entries.english: Whatever inferior states exist in my mental continuum until Nibbāna,

entries.pali: Nassantu sabbadā yeva Yattha jāto bhave bhave
entries.english: may they always disappear, wherever I am reborn, life after life.
May I have a straight mind, mindfulness and wisdom, and purification through effacement and energy.

entries.pali: Mārā labhantu nokāsaṃ Kātuñca vīriyesu me
entries.english: May Māra find no opportunity to obstruct my efforts.

entries.pali: Buddho dīpavaro nātho Dhammo nātho varuttamo
entries.english: The Buddha, the excellent lamp, is my refuge; the Dhamma is my supreme refuge.

entries.pali: Nātho paccekabuddho ca Saṅgho nāthottaro mamaṃ
entries.english: The Paccekabuddha is my refuge, and the Saṅgha is my highest refuge.
By the power of these supreme ones, may Māra gain no opportunity [over me].
Now let us recite the Daily Reflections.

entries.pali: Jarādhammomhi jarāṃ anatīto; for ladies: anatītā.
entries.english: I am of the nature to age; I have not gone beyond aging.

entries.pali: Byādhidhammomhi byādhiṃ anatīto; for ladies: anatītā.
entries.english: I am of the nature to be ill; I have not gone beyond illness.

entries.pali: Maraṇadhammomhi maraṇaṃ anatīto; for ladies: anatītā.
entries.english: I am of the nature to die; I have not gone beyond death.

entries.pali: Sabbehi me piyehi manāpehi nānābhāvo vinābhāvo
entries.english: All that is mine, dear and delightful, will change and be separated from me.
I am the owner of my kamma; the heir to my kamma.
Born of my kamma; related to my kamma.
I rely on my kamma.

entries.pali: Tassa dāyādo; for ladies: dāyādā. bhavissāmi
entries.english: Of that I shall be the heir.
For translation, see page sec:m13 (Morning Chanting)
Jong-ben-suk ben-suk-terd. Ya-dai-mee-wehn gae-gan-lae-gan-leuy.
Jong-ben-suk-ben-suk-terd, ya-dai-biat-bian-seung-gan lae-gan-leuy.
Jong-ben-suk-ben-suk-terd, ya-dai-mee-kwam-tuk-gai-tuk-jai-leuy.
Jong-mee-kwam-suk-gai suk-jai, rak-sa-ton-hai-pon jak-tuk-pai, tang-sin-terd.
Sat-tang-lai, tee-gerd-pen aṇ-ḍa-ja, tee-gerd-pen ja-lā-bu-ja, tee-gerd-pen saṃ-se-da-ja, tee-gerd-pen o-pa-pāti-ka, jong-ma-rap-gu-son-pohn boon, hai-tuan-tua tuk-tua-tuern.

chapters.title: Ratanattayanāmakārapātha
footerNotes: This saying shows respect for the Triple Gem, firms faith in it, and keeps a close relationship with it in mind. It serves as a preliminary step that will make the attainment of Dhammakaya (the Body of Truth) more accessible.

entries.pali: Arahaṃ sammāsambuddho bhagavā, buddhaṃ bhagavantaṃ abhivādemi.

entries.english: The Buddha is my refuge.”
The Dhamma is my refuge.”
The Saṅgha is my refuge.”

chapters.title: Homage to the Great Master Phramongkolthepmuni
chapters.titleEnglish: Homage to the Great Master Phramongkolthepmuni

entries.pali: Gom grahb ma nat nohm wa ra jom wi chaa jarn
entries.english: With reverent devotion, we bow in homage to the Great Master.

entries.pali: Nop ong pra song yaan chi na boot chi noh dome
entries.english: We honor the noble one, a true heir to the Victor's (Jina's) way.

entries.pali: Aek song pra naam jan- ta-sa-roh wi sut som
entries.english: Supreme and singular, pure and radiant in virtue.

entries.pali: Tuay tep ma nut prom a pi wan ta naa gaan
entries.english: Honored and revered by devas, humans, and Brahmās.

entries.pali: Taan wang wi mut ponh chana gohn pa yaa marn
entries.english: He opened the way to liberation, triumphing over the Lord of Māra.

entries.pali: Mung sut na ruh paan a ti yaan pra nam chai
entries.english: He strives toward Nibbāna, guided by noble wisdom.

entries.pali: Plea sheep ta wai saat mu ni naat na pen sai
entries.english: He upheld the true discipline, the Sage beyond reproach.

entries.pali: Yoot ning sa nit nai ha ruh tai na glang gai
entries.english: Steadfast and composed, firm in heart and mind.

entries.pali: Duang tam sa waang lam pah hu tammagai prai
entries.english: The light of Dhamma shines forth, illuminating every path.

entries.pali: Hen sut da lort saai wa ra gai wi set saan
entries.english: With sublime insight, he sees clearly through all realms.

entries.pali: witcha pra chaan chiaow ma na diaow pa jone marn
entries.english: Master of profound knowledge, single-minded against defilements.

entries.pali: Braab sin gi laet raan a pi barn ma ha chon
entries.english: He uprooted unwholesome states, bringing benefit to the multitude.

entries.pali: Ruu jaeng gra jang jin pra ta win ja ruam pohn
entries.english: Clearly knowing cause and effect, he unites wisdom with fruition.

entries.pali: Yoot ningh lu makh pohn a nu sohn pra toan tan
entries.english: Abiding firmly in attainment, worthy of constant recollection.

entries.pali: Yom dai mi yom pae ma na nae mi prae pan
entries.english: Never defeated, never shaken by delusion.

entries.pali: Gram suek tuk kern wan sa la plun u tit plea
entries.english: Waging the daily battle, he gave himself for the good of all.

entries.pali: Jai taan mi wan wai ja ka yai pra saat sri
entries.english: With unwavering heart, he upheld the noble dispensation.

entries.pali: Saang pra lae kon dee ku na mee ta lort chon
entries.english: He nurtured noble ones and good people with boundless compassion.

entries.pali: Duay dae cha sanh sern su ja rern pi pat pohn
entries.english: May his virtues bring growth, prosperity, and success.

entries.pali: Koh pohn pra mong kohn taep mu nee pi chit marn
entries.english: Through the blessing of Phramongkolthepmuni, conqueror of Māra.

entries.pali: Uai chai ma lai sohk ni ra roak lu pai parn
entries.english: May hearts be filled with joy, free from illness and danger.

entries.pali: Soab suk ga sem sarn ta na jak kra paht mee
entries.english: May happiness and well-being prevail throughout the land.

entries.pali: Ruu jaeng pra taam maa lu wi cha pra chin na see
entries.english: May insight into Dhamma and true knowledge arise.

entries.pali: Biam boon ya baa ra mee suk ka san ni ran gaan
entries.english: May merit and perfection flourish, bringing lasting peace.
